title: [FOLDED → OOM-0036] Abort: `PyTraceBack_Here` appends to a non-traceback `__traceback__` under OOM (`traceback.c:313`)
notes: RETIRED 2026-06-24 — folded into OOM-0036. This `traceback.c:313` `_PyTraceBack_FromFrame` assert (`tb_next` is neither NULL nor a traceback) was a DOWNSTREAM DETECTOR FACE of OOM-0036's `_CALL_LIST_APPEND` `list.append` double-free under MemoryError, not an independent bug. rr reverse-execution on this vehicle pinned the over-decref'd object (an inspect.Parameter) to `_PyList_AppendTakeRefListResize@Objects/listobject.c:531` <- `_CALL_LIST_APPEND` (generated_cases.c.h:3981); the appended item is freed by the append's resize-failure cleanup while a stale operand-stack _PyStackRef still points at it, and the traceback.c:313 assert fires when that double-freed item's reused storage is/aliases the in-flight exception's traceback (sibling faces: pycore_stackref.h:726 negref, tuple_alloc freelist SEGV). The traceback.c:313 dedup key now lives in OOM-0036's meta. Artifacts (vehicle_source.py, backtrace.txt, repro.py) retained here for provenance. The gen scripts skip status==folded entries (not counted, no keys emitted). See reports/OOM-0036-list-append-oom-double-free/ and catalog `84d78b6` (diagnostic) + the fold commit. id NOT reused.
